interactions.summary: Jiang argues the ground-invasion phase has already begun through a downed F-15 rescue operation; he says the air war cannot be sustained indefinitely, Iran is more creative than expected, and U.S. special-operations culture turns military action into optics, books, and movies.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Sneako asks or frames the issue; Jiang answers in the main analytic voice where present.

speaker_notes.note: Machine diarization is consistent in this packet: SPEAKER_00 is Sneako/interviewer and SPEAKER_01 is Jiang, except where intro/outro material is host-only.

claims.claim: Jiang says a pilot rescue after an F-15 shootdown already functioned as a ground incursion and will set a precedent for more ground operations.
He argues U.S. jets and air campaigns are not built for a long war; maintenance, losses, and air defenses will push America toward ground forces.
He says American special-operations culture privileges optics, narrative, book deals, and movie deals over sober military strategy.

signature_moments.moment: The rescue mission is treated as theater: war becomes the path to a book deal or movie deal.
signature_moments.source_phrase: can i get a book deal can i get a movie deal
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the episode’s sharpest critique of American military celebrity culture.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A war logic where operations are chosen for narrative spectacle and later media value rather than strategic payoff.
